intro: Angelica sinensis, also known as Dong Quai, is a traditional Chinese medicinal herb commonly used to promote women's health and relieve menstrual discomfort. The capsules made from this herb are often taken to alleviate symptoms such as bloating, cramps, and mood swings associated with premenstrual syndrome. However, it's essential to consult a healthcare professional before using Angelica sinensis capsules, especially if you have any underlying medical conditions or are taking other medications.
side_effects: Angelica sinensis capsule can have side effects such as gastrointestinal upset, allergic reactions, and interactions with certain medications, particularly blood thinners and diabetes medications. These side effects may be exacerbated in individuals with certain medical conditions or when taking the capsule in high doses. It is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using angelica sinensis capsule, especially if you are pregnant, breastfeeding, or have a history of bleeding disorders.
uses: Angelica sinensis capsule is used to alleviate various symptoms associated with menopausal and menstrual issues, such as hot flashes and irregular periods. It also exhibits anti-inflammatory properties and is used to treat conditions like arthritis and bronchitis. Additionally, it may help to boost the immune system and reduce fatigue.
heavy_periods: Angelica sinensis capsule, also known as dong quai, has been traditionally used to help alleviate heavy menstrual bleeding due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties. Studies suggest that it may help regulate menstrual cycles and reduce menstrual flow, but more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness. However, it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using angelica sinensis capsule for heavy periods.
fever: Angelica sinensis capsule is often used in traditional Chinese medicine to help reduce fever and alleviate symptoms associated with colds and flu. It is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties, making it a popular remedy for fever relief. However, it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before taking any herbal supplement.
recipe: To make angelica sinensis capsule for medicinal use, the first step is to dry and clean the angelica sinensis roots and leaves, and then grind them into a fine powder. The powder is then mixed with a suitable excipient, such as starch or talc, to create a uniform blend. Finally, the blend is filled into gelatin capsules or hard shell capsules and sealed properly to ensure the medicinal properties are preserved.
taste: Angelica sinensis capsule has a slightly bitter and earthy taste, often compared to a combination of licorice and ginseng. The bitterness is typically mild, and the earthy flavor is often described as rich and herbal. The overall taste is often described as a natural and earthy flavor profile.
pregancy: Angelica sinensis capsule is not typically recommended for pregnant women, as it may stimulate the uterus and cause contractions. Taking angelica sinensis capsule during pregnancy may also increase the risk of bleeding and other complications. It's essential for pregnant women to consult their healthcare provider before using any herbal supplements.
tea: Angelica sinensis capsule tea, also known as dong quai tea, is a herbal tea made from the roots of the Angelica sinensis plant. It has been traditionally used in Chinese medicine to promote women's health, reduce menstrual cramps, and improve overall well-being. Drinking angelica sinensis capsule tea may help to relax the body and promote a restful sleep.
for: Angelica sinensis capsule is traditionally used in Chinese medicine to promote blood circulation and reduce blood pressure. It is believed to have a cooling effect on the body, helping to alleviate symptoms of fever and inflammation. Additionally, angelica sinensis capsule is thought to have a protective effect on the cardiovascular system, potentially lowering the risk of heart disease.

benefits_skin: Angelica sinensis capsule benefits for skin include improving skin elasticity and reducing the appearance of fine lines and wrinkles, resulting in a smoother and more youthful complexion. It is also known to enhance skin radiance and reduce inflammation, promoting overall skin health and well-being. blood_pressure: Angelica sinensis capsule may have a potential effect on lowering blood pressure due to its diuretic and vasodilatory properties. However, more research is needed to confirm its efficacy in managing hypertension. It is essential to consult a healthcare professional before using Angelica sinensis capsule as a treatment for high blood pressure.
properties: Angelica sinensis capsule has therapeutic properties such as improving blood circulation, reducing inflammation, and acting as an antioxidant to promote overall health and well-being. It is often used to treat conditions like anemia, fatigue, and menstrual disorders. Additionally, it may help to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels.
plant: Angelica sinensis capsule is a type of herbal supplement derived from the plant Angelica sinensis, also known as dong quai. This plant is native to China and has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for centuries to treat various health issues, including menstrual problems and menopausal symptoms. The capsules are often used to promote overall well-being and are believed to have anti-inflammatory properties.
bags: Angelica sinensis capsule bags are a type of traditional Chinese medicine, often used to promote overall health and well-being. These bags typically contain dried Angelica sinensis roots, also known as dong quai, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. When consumed as a tea or capsule, the roots are thought to help alleviate symptoms of menstrual cramps, fatigue, and other health issues.
bath: Angelica sinensis capsule bath, also known as dong quai bath, is a traditional Chinese herbal bath treatment that combines the soothing properties of angelica sinensis with warm water to promote relaxation and reduce stress. The capsules typically contain dried and powdered angelica sinensis root, which is released into the water as they dissolve, creating a fragrant and calming bath experience. This herbal bath is believed to have various health benefits, including improving circulation, reducing inflammation, and promoting overall well-being.
blend: Angelica sinensis capsule blends are often used in traditional Chinese medicine to promote relaxation and balance the body's energy. They typically combine Angelica sinensis, also known as dong quai, with other herbal ingredients to enhance its health benefits. This blend may help alleviate symptoms of menopause, menstrual cramps, and other women's health issues.
blend recipe: Angelica sinensis capsule typically combines the dried roots of Angelica sinensis with other herbs and ingredients such as Astragalus membranaceus, Atractylodes macrocephala, and Paeonia lactiflora to create a balanced blend. The traditional ratio of each ingredient may vary depending on the specific recipe and the intended use of the blend. This combination is often used in traditional Chinese medicine to support digestive health and boost the immune system.
leaves: Angelica sinensis capsule leaves, also known as dong quai, are commonly used in traditional Chinese medicine for their potential health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving circulation. The leaves contain compounds that may have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which could help to alleviate various health conditions. However, more research is needed to fully understand the effects of angelica sinensis capsule leaves on human health.
dosage: Angelica sinensis capsule dosages may vary depending on the specific brand and intended use, but a typical dose is often recommended to be between 1-3 grams per day. It's essential to consult with a healthcare professional to determine the best dosage for individual needs. Additionally, some sources suggest taking the capsules after meals to minimize potential side effects.
dried: Angelica sinensis capsule dried, also known as Huang Qi capsule, is a traditional Chinese medicine made from the root of the Angelica sinensis plant, which has been used to promote energy and vitality. Dried Angelica sinensis capsules are often used to support the immune system and alleviate symptoms of stress and fatigue. They may also be used to improve digestion and relieve menstrual cramps in women.
breastfeeding: Angelica sinensis capsule may be used in traditional Chinese medicine to potentially increase milk production in breastfeeding women, but its effectiveness and safety have not been extensively scientifically proven. Some studies suggest that it may have a stimulating effect on the body's lactation process. However, more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and potential risks associated with its use.
children: Angelica sinensis capsule children, when referring to infants taking the capsule form of the herb, are generally advised to be at least 3 years old due to potential digestive issues. However, it's essential to consult a pediatrician or healthcare professional before administering any supplement to children. Parents should also be cautious and monitor their child's behavior and reactions after consuming the capsule.
before_bed: Angelica sinensis capsule is often taken before bed as it may help promote relaxation and reduce stress levels due to its potential sedative effects. The capsule, also known as dong quai, is believed to promote better sleep quality and duration by regulating the body's circadian rhythms. Additionally, it may also help alleviate symptoms of insomnia and promote a restful night's sleep.
sleepy: Angelica sinensis capsule is often used to promote relaxation and reduce stress, which may help with falling asleep. However, individual results may vary, and it's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using any supplement. Taking Angelica sinensis capsule as directed, in combination with a consistent sleep schedule and a healthy lifestyle, may contribute to improved sleep quality.
daily: Angelica sinensis capsule is often taken daily as part of traditional Chinese medicine to support overall health and well-being. It is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to improve circulation and reduce stress. Regular consumption of angelica sinensis capsule may also help to boost the immune system and promote relaxation.
and: Angelica sinensis capsule, also known as dong quai, is a popular herbal supplement traditionally used to support women's health and alleviate menstrual symptoms. It is often combined with other teas, such as peppermint and chamomile, to create soothing and calming blends. Additionally, ginseng tea and licorice root tea are also commonly consumed for their perceived health benefits.
caffeine: Angelica sinensis capsule typically contains a negligible amount of caffeine, as it is derived from the roots of the angelica plant, which is naturally caffeine-free. However, some commercial products may contain added caffeine or other ingredients. The primary active compounds in angelica sinensis capsule are considered to be various flavonoids, polysaccharides, and other bioactive substances.
buy: Angelica sinensis capsule can be purchased from various online retailers and health supplement stores, often in capsule or tablet form. These capsules typically contain dried and processed extracts of the Angelica sinensis root. When buying angelica sinensis capsules, it's essential to choose a reputable brand and follow the recommended dosage instructions.
